task_completion.description: Import the lever_arm.png sprite into OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz xsheet, timeline, or level strip shows 'lever_arm' loaded, and the mechanical arm sprite is visible in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: Move the pivot point to the center of the hinge circle at the top of the arm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Using the Animate tool, the center/pivot point indicator (a small circle with crosshairs) is visibly moved from the center of the image to the top hinge circle of the mechanical arm.

task_completion.description: Set a keyframe at Frame 1 with 0 degrees rotation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The timeline or xsheet shows a keyframe at frame 1, and the lever arm is in its original vertical orientation (0 degrees) in the viewer.

task_completion.description: Animate a rotation to -90 degrees at Frame 20.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The timeline or xsheet shows a keyframe at frame 20. In the viewer, the arm is rotated 90 degrees (horizontal). Because the pivot is at the top hinge, the arm should appear to have swung from the top, rather than spinning in place around its center.

task_completion.description: Save the scene as pivot_rig.tnz in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Scene' dialog shows the filename 'pivot_rig.tnz' in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/projects/' directory, or the OpenToonz main window title bar updates to show 'pivot_rig.tnz'.

task_completion.description: Render Frame 20 to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog shows the save path '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/pivot_test/' and the name 'frame', or a render progress bar/file manager confirms 'frame_0020.png' was generated.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to rig and animate the sprite.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with OpenToonz tools (like the Animate tool, xsheet, and viewer) rather than bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: The rotation is genuinely animated using keyframes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The xsheet/timeline shows interpolation or keyframe markers between frame 1 and frame 20, proving it's an animation and not just a statically rotated image.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to fake the outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal commands using ImageMagick, Python, or cp to artificially generate 'frame_0020.png' or 'pivot_rig.tnz' without using OpenToonz.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output render path: /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/pivot_test/frame_0020.png. Expected scene path: /home/ga/OpenToonz/projects/pivot_rig.tnz. A correct pivot adjustment means the arm rotates around the top hinge. This displaces the arm's centroid significantly (expected >80 pixels displacement). If the arm simply spins around its own center, the pivot was not correctly moved.
